title: Child Drowning Mortality And Supervision Gaps In South Asia
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring child drowning mortality risk and supervision gaps in South Asia, linking survey data, water-body mapping, and childcare-access data at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: Drowning kills over 230,000 people annually, with children under five accounting for the highest rate. Over 90 percent of drowning deaths occur in LMICs, with South Asia having the highest absolute numbers — Bangladesh alone records over 14,000 child drowning deaths annually. Water-body proximity maps and childcare-access data exist but are not linked to drowning-mortality data at sub-national scale.

success_metrics.target: Every drowning-risk estimate links survey, water-body, and childcare data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
